message:
feat(fleet): add fleet agent polling mode for autonomous action application

Add a new `fleet agent` subcommand that runs as a long-lived polling
loop, periodically reading the fleet transport layer for pending actions
and applying them to the local node:

- cli.rs: add FleetCommand::Agent variant with FleetAgentArgs struct
  (node_id, zone, poll_interval_secs, max_cycles, json output)
- main.rs: implement run_fleet_agent() polling loop with
  FleetAgentPollResult reporting, node_id/zone_id validation,
  quarantine version tracking, and human/JSON output modes
- middleware.rs: clarify doc comment — this module serves the in-process
  control-plane catalog surface, not a live HTTP/gRPC transport
- fleet_cli_e2e.rs: add tests for fleet agent polling lifecycle
- beads: sync issue tracker state

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Companion architecture/governance docs were updated to match that truth model.

 {"id":"bd-721z","title":"[10.15] Add ambient-authority audit gate for control-plane modules.","description":"## Why This Exists
Hard Runtime Invariant #10 from Section 8.5 mandates "no ambient authority" — control-plane modules must not directly access network sockets, spawn OS processes, read wall-clock time, or perform filesystem I/O without going through capability-gated APIs provided by the asupersync correctness kernel. Ambient authority makes code untestable in deterministic lab environments, breaks cancellation propagation (network calls ignore Cx deadlines), and prevents the evidence ledger from recording causal chains. This bead adds a CI gate that audits control-plane modules for ambient authority usage and fails the build when violations are found outside an explicit, signed allowlist.

## What This Must Do
1. Implement `tools/lints/ambient_authority_gate.rs` — an analysis pass that:
   - Scans control-plane modules (`crates/franken-node/src/connector/`, `crates/franken-node/src/conformance/`) for direct usage of: `std::net::*`, `std::process::Command`, `std::time::Instant`/`SystemTime`, `std::fs::*`, `tokio::net::*`, `tokio::process::*`, `tokio::time::sleep`/`timeout` (without Cx), `tokio::spawn` (without region ownership).
   - Reads a signed allowlist (`docs/specs/ambient_authority_allowlist.toml`) with entries: module path, permitted ambient API, justification, signer, expiry date.
   - Emits structured JSON findings with per-module/per-call pass/fail/allowed status.
2. Author `docs/specs/ambient_authority_policy.md` defining:
   - The complete list of restricted ambient APIs.
   - The allowlist signing and review process.
   - The escalation path for new exceptions.
3. Generate `artifacts/10.15/ambient_authority_findings.json` containing per-module audit results.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Ambient network/spawn/time effects in restricted modules fail CI; allowlist is explicit and signed.
- The gate operates on actual import/usage analysis, not just `use` statements (catches `std::net::TcpStream::connect` even without a `use` import).
- Unsigned or expired allowlist entries are treated as violations.
- Adding a direct `tokio::spawn` in a connector module without an allowlist entry causes immediate CI failure.
- The findings JSON is consumed by the section gate (bd-20eg) and the observability dashboards (bd-3gnh).

## Testing & Logging Requirements
- **Unit tests**: Validate the gate against synthetic modules — one clean, one with direct `std::net` usage, one with allowlisted usage, one with expired allowlist.
- **Integration tests**: Run against the real crate and assert zero non-allowlisted violations.
- **Adversarial tests**: Inject `std::process::Command::new("rm")` into a connector module and assert detection. Test with a forged (unsigned) allowlist entry.
- **Structured logs**: Event codes `AMB-001` (module clean), `AMB-002` (ambient authority violation), `AMB-003` (allowlisted usage), `AMB-004` (allowlist entry expired/invalid). Include module path, API call site (file:line), and allowlist entry ID.

## Expected Artifacts
- `tools/lints/ambient_authority_gate.rs`
- `docs/specs/ambient_authority_policy.md`
- `artifacts/10.15/ambient_authority_findings.json`
- `artifacts/section_10_15/bd-721z/verification_evidence.json`
- `artifacts/section_10_15/bd-721z/verification_summary.md`

## Dependencies
- **Upstream**: None within 10.15 (standalone audit gate)
- **Downstream**: bd-20eg (section gate)","acceptance_criteria":"- Ambient network/spawn/time effects in restricted modules fail CI; allowlist is explicit and signed.","notes":"Taking over blocked bead for closure audit: ambient-authority gate deliverables + evidence refresh.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleHarbor","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:59.726628519Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T03:21:30.417739262Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T03:21:30.417712482Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-15","self-contained","test-obligations"],"comments":[{"id":284,"issue_id":"bd-721z","author":"MistyBridge","text":"Implemented ambient-authority gate module + conformance wiring + allowlist/policy docs + artifacts. Remote rch runs confirmed; full cargo validation currently blocked by upstream franken_engine compile failures (Rust edition/serde/signature API mismatches). Keeping bead in_progress pending upstream green.","created_at":"2026-02-20T20:07:18Z"}]}

Add helper parsing/rendering and unit tests for assurance/query filtering.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"EmeraldOwl","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:38:27.448802927Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-28T05:00:33.711927389Z","closed_at":"2026-02-28T05:00:33.711904837Z","close_reason":"Implemented in commit 867890f; rch validation attempted. rch cargo fmt --check passed; rch cargo check currently blocked by upstream frankenengine-engine compile error at static_semantics.rs:705 (pattern binding mode).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":285,"issue_id":"bd-731v","author":"RainyReef","text":"Implemented registry publish/search handlers in crates/franken-node/src/main.rs; added registry_command_tests module. rch validation: cargo fmt --check passed after formatting; cargo check -p frankenengine-node --lib --bins passed once with isolated CARGO_HOME/TARGET (exit 0), but repeated check/clippy attempts are intermittently failing with remote exit=137 / stale worker cancellations under cluster pressure.","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:13:47Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-770ml","title":"Track 3d: Add fleet agent mode — poll for and apply fleet actions","acceptance_criteria":"1. fleet agent runs as long-lived process polling for actions
2. fleet agent --once processes all pending actions and exits
3. Quarantine actions applied to local trust card registry
4. Release actions remove local quarantine state
5. Node status reported after each poll cycle
6. Graceful shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT
7. Config fleet.node_id and fleet.poll_interval_seconds work
8. Structured tracing for all applied actions","notes":"SIGNAL HANDLING: Graceful SIGTERM/SIGINT requires the ctrlc crate (add ctrlc = "3" to dependencies). Use ctrlc::set_handler to set a flag, check flag in poll loop.

Alternative: use std::sync::atomic::AtomicBool with a signal handler thread. This avoids the ctrlc dependency but is more complex. The ctrlc crate is pure Rust and compatible with #![forbid(unsafe_code)] so it is the preferred choice.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T14:20:34.207455726Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T15:56:47.283935104Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-770ml","depends_on_id":"bd-36j7m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T14:21:03.669366029Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-770ml","title":"Track 3d: Add fleet agent mode — poll for and apply fleet actions","acceptance_criteria":"1. fleet agent runs as long-lived process polling for actions
2. fleet agent --once processes all pending actions and exits
3. Quarantine actions applied to local trust card registry
4. Release actions remove local quarantine state
5. Node status reported after each poll cycle
6. Graceful shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT
7. Config fleet.node_id and fleet.poll_interval_seconds work
8. Structured tracing for all applied actions","notes":"SIGNAL HANDLING: Graceful SIGTERM/SIGINT requires the ctrlc crate (add ctrlc = "3" to dependencies). Use ctrlc::set_handler to set a flag, check flag in poll loop.

Alternative: use std::sync::atomic::AtomicBool with a signal handler thread. This avoids the ctrlc dependency but is more complex.  {"id":"bd-7mt","title":"[10.2] Add CI gate: compatibility implementations must cite spec section + fixture IDs; missing references fail review gates.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.2 — Compatibility Core

Why This Exists:
Compatibility core as strategic wedge: policy-visible behavior, divergence receipts, L1/L2 lockstep, and spec-first fixture governance.

Task Objective:
Add CI gate: compatibility implementations must cite spec section + fixture IDs; missing references fail review gates.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Implement with explicit success/failure invariants and deterministic behavior under normal, edge, and fault scenarios.
- Ensure outcomes are verifiable via automated tests and reproducible artifact outputs.

Expected Artifacts:
- Section-owned design/spec artifact at `docs/specs/section_10_2/bd-7mt_contract.md`, capturing decision rationale, invariants, and interface boundaries.
- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_2/bd-7mt/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_2/bd-7mt/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.

Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.2] Add CI gate: compatibility implementations must cite spec section + fixture IDs; missing references fail review gates.", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.2] Add CI gate: compatibility implementations must cite spec section + fixture IDs; missing references fail review gates." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.2] Add CI gate: compatibility implementations must cite spec section + fixture IDs; missing references fail review gates." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.2] Add CI gate: compatibility implementations must cite spec section + fixture IDs; missing references fail review gates." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.2] Add CI gate: compatibility implementations must cite spec section + fixture IDs; missing references fail review gates." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:44.719663372Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T10:03:25.554310012Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T10:03:25.554283553Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-2","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7mt","depends_on_id":"bd-80g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:05Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
